parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff is 6,600+ lines across 84 files — an unusually large scope for a patch series that introduces new retry infrastructure, error dispatch, job lifecycle changes, and deletes a core module. The highest-confidence risk is the complete removal of scripts/lib/process.mjs without verifiable proof that no remaining code path imports it at runtime; static import checks (check-imports.mjs) do not cover dynamic imports or indirect references. Secondary risks include untested job-control and tracked-jobs changes that govern job state transitions.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs may leave dangling imports at runtime
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was entirely deleted in this diff. scripts/check-imports.mjs was updated (5 lines changed), suggesting awareness. However, a static import checker cannot catch dynamic import() calls, eval-based loading, configuration-driven module resolution, or references embedded in template strings. If any surviving file does `import ... from './process.mjs'` or `import('./lib/process.mjs')`, it will crash at runtime with MODULE_NOT_FOUND — and because the companion runs as a CLI tool, this would be a user-visible hard failure with no graceful degradation. The risk is elevated because the companion script (glm-companion.mjs) had 139 lines changed and render.mjs had 79 lines changed, both of which are likely consumers of process utilities.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for 'process\.mjs' (including dynamic imports, string concatenation, and config files) to confirm zero references remain. If any exist, either restore the module with a deprecation shim or migrate the callers before shipping.

findings.title: Job lifecycle modules changed without dedicated test coverage
findings.body: scripts/lib/job-control.mjs (20 lines changed) and scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) govern job state transitions — submission, tracking, and presumably cancellation or failure handling. Neither module has a dedicated test file in the tests/ directory. While job-render.test.mjs and result-propagation.test.mjs may exercise some paths, job state bugs are classically subtle: partial writes, orphaned job records, and race conditions on concurrent invocations. The tracked-jobs module being 78 lines changed with no direct test is a gap that could hide silent job-loss regressions.
findings.recommendation: Add a dedicated test file (tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs) covering: job creation, concurrent job tracking, cleanup on failure, and recovery from partially-written state. At minimum, run existing tests with a mutation on tracked-jobs to verify coverage.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs core client heavily modified with retry integration — no integration test for retry-on-real-failure path
findings.body: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) is the API client. A new retry module (retry.mjs, 203 lines) and error dispatch module (bigmodel-errors.mjs, 168 lines) were added. While retry.test.mjs (352 lines) exists as a unit test, the critical failure path is the integration: what happens when the client receives a retriable error, the retry fires, and the second attempt returns a different error class? The retry-and-client interaction is where idempotency gaps and double-submission bugs live. There is no integration test file visible that exercises the full retry→client→error-dispatch chain with mocked network failures.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration test that mocks the HTTP layer to return retriable errors (429, 503, network timeout) and verifies: (a) correct retry count, (b) no double-submission on non-idempotent endpoints, (c) correct error class propagation after retries exhaust. This is the most likely place for a silent user-facing regression.

next_steps: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/ commands/` to confirm no surviving imports reference the deleted module.
Verify that tracked-jobs.mjs and job-control.mjs are exercised by existing tests via coverage reporting (`node --test --experimental-test-coverage`).
Add an integration test for the retry→client→error-dispatch chain before tagging v0.4.7.
Consider whether the 84-file, 6,600-line diff scope is appropriate for a 0.4.x patch release or should be segmented.
